criteria.label: Write a complete `pyproject.toml` from scratch for a small library.
criteria.example: Author build-system, project (name/version/deps/scripts/extras), one tool.setuptools.packages.find, and one tool.pytest.ini_options block; `pip install -e '.[dev]'` succeeds.

criteria.label: Build sdist + wheel and inspect them.
criteria.example: `python -m build`, then `unzip -l dist/myapp-*.whl` shows the expected modules and `dist/myapp-*.tar.gz` contains the source tree without venv/test artefacts.

criteria.label: Switch the build backend without breaking the rest of pyproject.
criteria.example: Replace setuptools with hatchling: only `[build-system]` and `[tool.setuptools.*]`→`[tool.hatch.*]` change; everything builds and installs as before.

criteria.label: Expose a CLI command via `[project.scripts]`.
criteria.example: Add `mycli = 'mypkg.cli:main'`, install, run `mycli --help` from a fresh shell. The command lives at `<venv>/bin/mycli`.

criteria.label: Manage version from a single source of truth.
criteria.example: Use `dynamic = ['version']` + `setuptools-scm` reading git tags; release-tagging the repo updates the version on next build.

criteria.label: Distinguish library-style ranges from app-style pins.
criteria.example: Library `pyproject.toml`: `httpx>=0.27,<1`. Application: same plus `requirements.lock` with `httpx==0.27.2`; explain when each applies.
